title: CanonicalEvent
description: Canonical event schema — the foundational, extensible event structure for the Agent Evidence Protocol. Maps onto the AEP record model and provides a single-event envelope that adapters can produce from heterogeneous upstream event formats.

schema_version.description: Schema version discriminator. Consumers MUST reject unknown values.

event_id.description: Unique identifier for this event within its trace (UUIDv7 or equivalent).

event_type.description: Qualified event type name, e.g. 'tool.invocation', 'capability.decision', 'verifier.result'. Dot-separated namespace convention.

timestamp_ms.description: Epoch-millisecond timestamp when the event occurred.

source.description: Provenance: identifies the agent run that produced this event.

run_id.description: Unique identifier for the agent run.

parent_event_id.description: Parent event in a causal chain, if any.

payload.description: Event-type-specific body. Schema is determined by event_type; consumers should dispatch on that field. This field is extensible and intentionally untyped at the protocol level.

causal_chain_id.description: Groups events sharing a causal relationship.

security.description: Security and attestation metadata attached to this event.

taint_labels.description: Data-flow taint labels propagated from inputs.

capability_decision.description: Capability decision governing this event, if applicable.
